BACXD · Client Design · Return Brief

Welcome back, Christina.

This document is meant to orient you, not overwhelm you. Read it at whatever pace feels right, and everything in it is something we can talk through together.
Prepared by Mirella G. Smartt
Return date ~July 1, 2026
Your role Lead Product Designer · T5
Team BACXD · Client Design
A note from Mirella
Before anything else
Coming back from parental leave is its own kind of transition, separate from anything work-related. I want you to know that I am not expecting you to hit the ground running on day one. The work will be here. What matters more to me in the first couple of weeks is that you feel oriented, supported, and clear on what is yours to own, so you can ease back in on your own terms.
You are also returning to a changed team. Julie has moved on and I am your new manager. I want to be honest about that rather than gloss over it. I have a lot of context on the work, I have been building it since joining in May, and I genuinely want to earn your trust over time, not assume it. This document is one small step in that direction: giving you the full picture so you are not piecing it together from Slack and calendar invites.
My door, or Slack message, or Monday 1:1, is always open. There are no wrong questions here.
A little about me
I am based in Austin, TX, though I originally hail from Hamburg, Germany and have lived all over the United States along the way: Florida, Massachusetts, California, Nevada, and now Texas. Married to Erik, and mom/stepmom to Jack (19), stepson Max (17), and Lily (10). We also have a full house of cats: Mochi (calico), Choco (black), and BK (long-haired).

Outside of work I am an avid reader, anime aficionado, crafter, baker, and lover of color and fashion. I think you will find I bring that same enthusiasm for craft and color into how I think about design leadership. Looking forward to getting to know you too.
Org Context
What has changed since you left
A few things are meaningfully different from when you went on leave. Here is the honest summary.
New Leadership
Gray Knowlton joined as SVP Products (GM), he is Ava's boss and brings a strong, clear point of view on how the team operates. His framing is that we are building a system for coordinated action, hitting goals, and making products people actually want to use. He is direct about what he observed when joining (siloed progress, internal friction, legacy complexity) and equally direct about the opportunity. He is invested in leadership development and team culture as intentional priorities, not just outcomes. His All Hands deck is worth reading when you have 15 minutes.
New Team Structure
Whitney Perez shifted the PM structure from initiative-based to outcome-based ownership. The three frames are now Activate (Sean Rowland, PM), Engage (Erin Bentel, PM), and Grow (James McDade, PM). This is still being actively worked through, and designer and PM assignments are not yet fully finalized. Some of what is in the Portfolio Dashboard may still shift as the product org structure is confirmed. Your scope sits within the MVIP/RCC migration work you know well, and we will walk through exactly what that means in our first 1:1.
Work That Has Continued
The MVIP/RCC migration has continued. Most of the in-flight features you knew are in development or recently shipped. Julie documented the handoff thoroughly before her departure and the context is preserved. I will walk you through exactly what is yours to pick up, nothing should feel like you are walking into a mystery.
Your Scope
What is yours when you return
You are returning to the work you know well: MVIP/RCC migration in RealPro Dashboard. This is familiar territory and the handoff from Julie is documented and clean. Here is the full picture of what is in flight and what is transferring to you.
MVIP/RCC Migration
Where the migration stands
Almost all migrated features now in flight
  • Core design work is largely complete
  • Feedback loop in progress with Beta clients, led by Research: Tim Ballew
  • Target: MVIP client migration complete end of Q3 FY27
  • PM: Charlie Lehman · Eng leads: James Monsebroten, Ryan Saathoff · TPM: Dee (Diana) Pena
Key Risk
The data, not the design
  • Biggest remaining risk is client data, not feature design
  • Clients have duplicate accounts, missing Party IDs and Broker Op IDs, and outdated contact data
  • This makes linking FocusFire and Salesforce accounts technically risky
  • No design plan yet for communicating legacy portal deprecation to clients. Likely yours to own on return
Features Transferring to You
Feature Target Status Notes
ISA role FY27 Q1 In development Transferring from Julie
Agent removal FY27 Q1 In development Transferring from Julie
Prospect and PCL assignment / status updates FY27 Q1 In development Transferring from Julie to Jon
Legacy portal deprecation comms Before Q3 No owner yet Opcity Broker Portal + MVIP Portal sunset. Likely yours to own
Live Transfer Agent Assignment
PM: Charlie Lehman · Q1 discovery complete · Q2 hypothesis testing
Picking up where Julie left off
  • Addresses a growing problem: as live transfer volume increases, clients have limited control over which agent receives a live transfer lead
  • Discovery scoped across the full ~600 MVIP client base before any build commitment
  • H2 build is conditional on Q2 test results
  • Key constraint: agent assignment logic lives in FocusFire, which is on the deprecation path. Eng coordination with Daniel and eng LT needed to confirm which system to build in for test and scale
  • Full brief and all context ready to hand over. Jon Grimes is also on this brief alongside you

Key Cross-Functional Partners
Charlie Lehman
PM · MVIP/RCC Migration + Live Transfer Agent Assignment
Your primary PM for the work you are picking up. Q1 discovery is complete; connect with him early to get context on where Q2 hypothesis testing stands.
Erin Bentel
Sr. PM · Engage capability (was RPD in FY26)
Grounds work in clear outcomes and user-problem definition. Strong design partnership instincts. You will likely interact with her as the org structure settles.
James Monsebroten
Engineering Manager · 25 years at RDC
Carries the institutional knowledge of the whole platform. Make sure he is in the room for anything high-stakes. When he is not available, flag it rather than working around it.
Dee (Diana) Pena
Technical Program Manager
Keeps the RPD train on the tracks. First person to loop in when you need to schedule a cross-functional review or get the right engineering stakeholders in the room.
Tim Ballew
Research Lead · MVIP/RCC
Leading the Beta client feedback loop. Good to connect with early to understand what signals are coming in from research and what design questions are still open.
Whitney Perez
Director, Product
New and actively building her strategic vision. Thinks big with strong philosophical direction. Pooja and Will are good partners in bringing structure and clarity here.
The Bigger Picture
Your work feeds into the platform's Activate capability: first client value and frictionless early success. The MVIP migration is the largest client re-onboarding the platform has had, and the design work you are picking up sits at the center of it. We will connect the individual features to that broader frame in our early 1:1s so the context is useful, not just a label on top of the work.
FY27 Goals
Your goals, and where things actually stand
Here's the honest version: leadership needed goals entered in the system by 6/30, so we went ahead and entered yours on your behalf, based on what the rest of the team is working toward. Broadly speaking, everyone's goals follow the same shape right now, so nothing in there should feel out of left field. We'll walk through them together so you know exactly what's in there and why.
Already done
Goals entered to meet the HR deadlineBuilt from the team's shared direction so we could hit the 6/30 system deadline while you were out.
Week 2–3
Walk through them together in our 1:1sWe'll go through what's in the system and what it means for your work, so you have the full picture.
What Goals at Lead Level Look Like
At T5, goals tend to span three areas: craft (the quality and depth of your design work), influence (how you shape the work of others, including juniors and cross-functional partners), and growth (what you are building toward). I have a Career Growth Tracker set up for the whole team that maps this out, we will look at it together in one of our early 1:1s.

How We Work Together
Meeting cadence and how to reach me
Here is what our regular rhythm looks like. Everything is new, I have not had the chance to set up all of these with you yet, so consider this a preview of what we will confirm together.
1:1 with Mirella
Mondays, frequency and length TBD together. This is your time and I follow your agenda. Bring whatever is on your mind: work, growth, concerns, questions. I will use it for feedback and context-sharing too, but you set the direction.
BACXD Team Meeting
Tuesdays, weekly with your direct team (Client Design). Mix of updates, design crits, and team discussions. You will be a senior voice in the room and I value your perspective on cross-team topics, not just your own work.
Full BACXD Sync
Tuesdays, bi-weekly with Ava's full BACXD team across all design disciplines. Broader org visibility, cross-team updates, and alignment across the wider design organization.
RPD Design Sync
Fridays 1:30pm CT, weekly design sync for the RPD team. This is where in-progress work gets shared and reviewed.
UWP Requirements Review
Tuesdays 2pm CT, weekly with Engineering. This is the primary sync for aligning design decisions with eng constraints.
Slack
Best way to reach me for quick questions or quick context. I am responsive and try to be clear about when something needs a longer conversation vs. a fast answer. I will add you to the relevant team Slack channels in your first week.
Office Hours
Tuesday through Friday at 12:20pm, optional drop-in time with a sign-up sheet. No agenda needed. Good for quick questions, a second opinion on work, or just getting some face time. I will share the sign-up link when you are back.
Team Rituals
Book Club, kicking off Tuesday, July 14
One more thing happening in the background: we started a book club as part of our bi-weekly Client Design Team Syncs. First session lands the week you're back, so no catch-up required, you'll be there from the start. Two books are on deck, one in progress and one lined up next.
[ ]
How Leadership
Actually Works
LARRY YATCH
We're discussing "How Leadership (Actually) Works" by Larry Yatch, a Navy SEAL's system for coordinating teams. Available on Amazon or Spotify. Three sessions, spread across our bi-weekly syncs:
01
Session #1 — July 14, 2026
Intro + Chapters 1–2 (Upside-Down Leadership / How Language Actually Works)
02
Session #2 — July 28, 2026
Chapters 3–5 (Pillars 1–3: Success, Team, Behavior)
03
Session #3 — August 11, 2026
Chapters 6–9 (Pillars 4–6 + Leadership Implemented + Conclusion)
Before You Buy
Please purchase both books yourself and submit an expense report in Concur, I will approve it. No need to wait on me to source or expense these.
[ ]
The Pyramid
Principle
BARBARA MINTO
"The Pyramid Principle" by Barbara Minto is our next book club read, on logic and structure in writing and thinking. Not yet scheduled — sessions and cadence to be confirmed once we wrap the Yatch book. Same ask applies: purchase and expense in Concur, I will approve.
Team Infrastructure
Tools and resources I have set up
I have built a few things for the team since joining. Here is what exists and how it is relevant to you.
Design Career Growth Tracker
Private · Password protected
A tool I built to manage career development across the team. Includes your career path visualization (current level vs. next level expectations from the RDC career ladder), skill ratings, a roadmap view of your work across quarters, review cycle stage tracking, and a space for 1:1 notes. Your password is christina_grow. We will look at it together in an early 1:1. It is a starting point for conversation, not a performance evaluation.

One small ask: I like to celebrate the team on birthdays and other occasions, so if you are comfortable sharing your birthday, address, phone number, and a personal email, there is a place to add those in the tracker. Completely optional.

Open Career Growth Tracker →
Team Survey
Quick · ~10 min
When I first joined, I asked the team to fill out a survey to help me understand how everyone was feeling, what was working, and what they needed from a manager. I would love for you to fill it out when you have 10 minutes. Your perspective as someone returning from leave and meeting a new manager is genuinely useful context. No right or wrong answers.

Take the survey →
Portfolio Dashboard
Reference
A comprehensive reference document covering the FY27 product strategy, surface roadmaps for RPD, RPA, and the Online Store, team structure, key decisions from the FY27 roadmap workshop, and a design strategy section specific to BACXD. Useful for getting up to speed on context quickly.

Note: designer and PM assignments reflected in the dashboard are still being finalized as the overall product org structure is confirmed. Some details may shift, and I will keep it updated as things are locked in.

Your Team
Who is on the team now
The team is the same people, with one change: Carolina Sanchez (contractor) has rolled off. Everyone else is here and looking forward to having you back.
Will Soemargo
Senior PD · Vancouver · Activate · RPD + RPA
Promoted out-of-cycle in January. Working on the Activate capability: onboarding, migration, and RPA foundation work. Strong collaborator; you two will likely overlap on RPD.
Pooja Sheth
Senior PD · Chicago · Engage · RPD + RPA
Eight-plus years at RDC with the deepest platform knowledge on the team. Wrapping LEX for Teams / Profiles carryover from FY26, then moving fully into Engage. PAC-nominated for Lead level.
Lauren Lang
Senior PD · Austin · Grow · Online Store
Hired with lead-level experience. Owns the Online Store and is doing exploratory work on Notifications vs. Tasks as a cross-surface design framework. Strong strategic instincts.
Jon Grimes
Mid-Level PD · Austin · Engage · RPD
Has been on MVIP migration work alongside you and is now picking up Engage work as Pooja wraps her FY26 carryover. He will hand off the admin/role work to you cleanly.
Org Direction
Where the organization is heading
A few things worth knowing about the broader direction so you have context for the decisions you will be making.
Platform Strategy
Unified platform, one login, one launchpad
  • ProDash is being sunset; RPD is the destination for all clients
  • ~600 MVIP clients migrating in H1, largest re-onboarding the platform has had
  • Core ProDash (C+, SPL, LEX) clients coming to RPD in H2, discovery starting now
AI Priority
Real Pro Assist, most important AI project of FY27
  • Intelligence layer that surfaces performance insights and coaching recommendations to agents
  • Leadership described it as the most important project of 2027
  • Thrive event demo is a live goal, design involvement in shaping the prototype is important
Commercial Model
Transitioning to Cost Per Lead (CPL)
  • Moving from fixed subscription slots to variable CPL pricing
  • Changes how products need to be explained in the dashboard and store
  • New pricing patterns will need design work in H2
Gray Knowlton's Framing, Worth Knowing
Gray (SVP Products, GM, Ava's boss) joined earlier this year with a clear philosophy: the team needs to operate as a system enabling coordinated action, not a collection of independent workstreams. His three success criteria are simple: operate with coordinated action, consistently hit goals, and build products people want to use every day. He is also explicit about wanting to invest in leadership development. For you as a Lead PD, that is a favorable environment. There is organizational appetite for designers who think and operate at a strategic level.
First Weeks Back
A suggested arc, not a prescription
There is no expectation that you hit the ground running. Here is how I am thinking about the first few weeks, but we will adjust this based on what actually feels right for you.
Wk 1
Reorient, no deliverables expected
Read through this brief at your own pace. Join the BACXD team meeting on Tuesday. Look through the Career Growth Tracker. Reach out to Jon to start getting back up to speed on the in-flight MVIP work. Ask whatever questions come up, nothing is a dumb question here.
Low pressure
Wk 1–2
Fill out the team survey
When you have 10 minutes, fill out the team survey. Your perspective matters, especially as someone returning from leave and meeting a new manager. Take the survey →
~10 minutes
Wk 2
First proper 1:1 with Mirella
Our Mondays are yours. First one will be a get-to-know-you conversation as much as a scope conversation. Bring whatever is on your mind. I will bring the Career Growth Tracker and we will look at it together.
Monday
Wk 2–3
Pick up the in-flight MVIP work
ISA role and agent removal are the most immediate handoffs. Jon will walk you through the work he has been covering. The brief for Live Transfer Agent Assignment (Charlie Lehman) will be ready and waiting.
Design WIP
Wk 2–3
Walk through your FY27 goals together
Goals were already entered to meet the 6/30 leadership deadline, based on the team's shared direction. We'll go through them together so you have the full picture.
Already entered
Wk 2–3
Submit your FY26 self-review
Since your manager review discussion already happened before you left, this is just the self-review write-up so I can attach it when I submit your review. Open the self-review doc →
Due July 31
Month 2
Legacy portal deprecation comms, design ownership
The Opcity Broker Portal and MVIP Portal are being sunset in Q3. No design plan exists yet for how clients are informed. This is an important piece of work that will likely be yours to own and we will scope it together once you are oriented.
Q3 deadline
I know there is a lot in this document. You do not need to absorb it all at once, and you definitely do not need to act on most of it in the first week. The goal was to give you the full picture so that nothing catches you by surprise, and so you can come to our first 1:1 with whatever is actually on your mind rather than spending that time on logistics.

You are coming back to a team that is glad you are here. The work is meaningful, the direction is clearer than it has been in a while, and you have a real opportunity to shape how design operates at a senior level on this team. I am looking forward to building that with you.
